rationale: Nails it: newNodeManager returns err on NodeIP-fetch timeout and newProxyServer propagates it, aborting startup vs prior non-fatal backoff+localhost fallback; names the cloud-provider (azure) late-IP regression.

Framed as a comment-scope issue but explicitly states the 5-min timeout now 'fatally bounds ... NodeIP readiness in ALL modes (previously a ~30-60s non-fatal wait)' — the fatal-vs-nonfatal regression per must_flag.
All exit sites now use klog.Flush()+os.Exit vs prior klog.FlushAndExit(ExitFlushTimeout,1); can drop the exit-reason log; ties to nojnhuh's post-merge cluster-creation breakage (h1).
server.go logs 'Successfully retrieved NodeIPs' unconditionally with no warn-on-empty — exactly danwinship's empty-NodeIPs logging concern (h2).

Order-sensitive reflect.DeepEqual over GetNodeHostIPs output with no debounce; normal dual-stack bringup (kubelet v4 then CCM adds v6) flips DeepEqual and calls exitFunc(1) -> crashloop — a real bug distinct from the by-design exit-on-change.

OnNodeChange sets n.node=node before GetNodeHostIPs validation; a transient IP-less update poisons the baseline (logs+returns) so a later IP-restore reads as a change -> spurious exit; error path also skips the change check.

Run() registers NodeConfig/NodeTopologyConfig handlers on the informer NewNodeManager already Started+synced; the async synthetic-Add can run handleNodeEvent with zero handlers (dedup then swallows initial zone) and races the unlocked eventHandlers append — old code deliberately Started after registration.

NodeEligible() upgraded RLock->Lock (guards no field it uses now) and calls Node() which DeepCopies the whole v1.Node on every /healthz+liveness probe to read DeletionTimestamp/Taints — a real perf regression this PR introduced.

s.podCIDRs = NodeManager.PodCIDRs() is now unconditional (was gated to LocalModeNodeCIDR in platformSetup); the '// only used for LocalModeNodeCIDR' field comment is now stale and the field is populated in all modes — a real diff-verifiable behavior change.

The new PollUntilContextCancel loop logs nothing per attempt, whereas deleted getNodeIPs logged each failure; during a multi-minute startup stall operators get zero visibility — a real observability regression.

NewNodeConfig registers only UpdateFunc/DeleteFunc (AddFunc dropped), so the synthetic Add-replay to the late-registered handler is swallowed and a drift in the poll->register gap can miss the crash-on-change net; also contradicts OnNodeChange's 'creation or modification' doc.

The only such comment ('start after ... configure the shared informer event handler first') was DELETED by this PR, not retained; and late-registration replay is exactly what creates the real c8 race, so calling it 'factually incorrect' misreads it.

Flags the exit-on-NodeIP-change / exit-on-delete which the type doc states is intentional (crash to restart with fresh config); factually accurate but targets by-design behavior — known_safe safe-exit-on-change.
True: no return after the watchPodCIDRs exitFunc(1), so it falls through to the NodeIP check — but inert in production (os.Exit never returns); only matters with an injected test exitFunc.

The (nil,nil) path is currently safe (immediate=true guarantees err set before any false,nil); framed as future fragility/implicit-invariant + misleading domain err on cancellation — a nitpick per the answer key, not a live panic.

hs.nodeManager is dereferenced with no nil guard, but it is non-nil by construction on the normal path (self-described as a 'landmine ... non-nil only by construction today') — fragility nitpick, not a refutable bug.

True: handleChangeNode (UpdateFunc-only) carries a DeletedFinalStateUnknown tombstone branch that only ever fires on the delete path — dead/misleading code copied from the delete handler, but harmless.

PodCIDRs() returns n.node.Spec.PodCIDRs and OnNodeChange stores the informer-owned *Node without DeepCopy while Node() DeepCopies — a real asymmetry, but informer objects are effectively read-only/replaced so practical aliasing risk is low.
True: NodeIPs() and OnNodeChange's oldNodeIPs discard the GetNodeHostIPs error via _, unlike the poll loop; conflates no-IPs with lookup-failed — low-value code-quality inconsistency.

True: NodeManager logs via package-global klog.InfoS/ErrorS rather than a ctx-scoped logger like sibling *Config types (which store klog.FromContext(ctx)); dropped contextual keys — style/consistency nit.
True: nodeLister is only used inside newNodeManager's poll loop and never read after construction, so it could be a local; harmless dead struct field.
True: NodeTopologyConfig.listerSynced is assigned handlerRegistration.HasSynced but never read (no Run()/sync-wait method) — dead state, trivial.

thisNodeInformerFactory.Start(NeverStop) is never stopped, so on the error returns the reflector goroutines leak and accumulate across test constructions — but on success the informer is meant to run for process life, and on prod error the process exits; low value.
SRP/architecture opinion: NodeManager owns informer+poller+snapshot+watchdog+NodeHandler and 'current node' lives in two places — subjective design critique, no defect.

True that healthcheck now imports pkg/proxy and holds concrete *proxy.NodeManager (dependency direction inverted vs old node.go->healthcheck) instead of a one-method interface — a coupling/design observation, not a functional defect.

handleNodeEvent publishes n.topologyLabels by reference, but it always allocates a fresh map and never mutates a published one, and runs on the single informer goroutine — safe by convention, no live race; robustness nit.

Producer filter (handleNodeEvent hardcodes LabelTopologyZone) and consumer (CategorizeEndpoints) are coupled only by the prose comment this PR added; a real drift risk but a design/coherence nit, not a defect.
True: the added topology.go comment has a double space ('to  watch other endpoint types or labels') — pure typo.
True: winkernel TODO says 'OnTopologyChanged' while the method is OnTopologyChange, and the TODO sits above the doc line so godoc renders it — trivial comment nit.

True: metaproxier/winkernel OnTopologyChange doc wording differs from the identical iptables/ipvs/nftables comments, and the interface doc's 'provides the observed change' implies a delta though it's the full label set — doc-consistency nit.
True: the NodeManager type doc is a garbled run-on ('...NodeIPs and PodCIDRs handles node watch events...', duplicated 'handles') and omits that delete crashes — grammar/doc nit.
True: NodeIPs()/PodCIDRs() docs say 'polled in NewNodeManager()' implying a snapshot, but OnNodeChange overwrites n.node so they return live values — misleading doc, no functional impact.
True: OnNodeSynced doc says 'all pre-existing Nodes have been reported' though the informer is single-node field-selected and there's no AddFunc; minor doc inaccuracy/style break.
True: the //issues.k8s.io/111321 rationale for restart-on-PodCIDR-change was deleted and the new NodeIP/delete crash triggers carry no rationale — knowledge-preservation nit.

True: NodeTopologyConfig DeleteFunc is a no-op while handleNodeEvent's doc says it handles Delete; but stale zone labels never materialize because OnNodeDelete exitFunc(1)s the process first — doc/coverage nit, masked.

Plausible: SyncNode was removed in favor of pull-based NodeEligible(), so a ProxyHealthServer type-doc item describing the SyncNode push model would be stale; the doc text isn't in the shown hunks so lower confidence.
NodeConfig's 'accepts set/add/remove via channels' struct doc is stale (informer-driven), but it's pre-existing and unchanged by this diff — out-of-scope nit (self-acknowledged).
TestCategorizeEndpoints param naming is in topology_test.go which is not in the diff and pre-existing — out-of-scope nit (self-acknowledged).
True the os.Exit paths emit only a klog line (no metric/Event), but the replaced NodePodCIDRHandler also only logged+FlushAndExit — an enhancement request, not a regression/defect.

TestProxyServer_platformSetup was deleted; but the behavior it guarded (podCIDRs nil outside LocalModeNodeCIDR) intentionally no longer holds, so its removal is expected — the residual point is a low-value coverage gap on the new wiring.
True: the topology mock records only the last value with no call counter and the callback fires per-event regardless of notification, so the require.Empty negatives are weak — test-quality nit.

True: the case commented 'region ... should not receive notification' also changes the zone (us-west-2a -> us-east-1b) so OnTopologyChange does fire; there is no pure region-only case — misleading test comment.
True: the zone-removal transition ({zone:X}->{}) is never tested; only add/change of the zone label is exercised — low-value coverage gap.
True: the test registers the informer handler before Start(), unlike production which registers on the already-started informer, so it never exercises the c8 race — test-fidelity nit.

True: TestNodeManagerNode pre-populates the node so immediate=true succeeds on the first check and the 1ns poll/timeout path is never exercised — but the test targets Node()/OnNodeChange, so low value.
True: after the void OnNodeChange call the test re-asserts require.NoError on the stale constructor err — a tautological assertion, trivial.
True: deleted Test_getNodeIPs covered a malformed 'invalid-ip' (a distinct GetNodeHostIPs error path) not reproduced in TestNewNodeManager — minor coverage gap.
True: every TestNodeManagerOnNodeChange case supplies valid updated IPs, so the GetNodeHostIPs-error early-return (where the c4 bug lives) is untested — coverage gap, but framed only as 'untested'.
True: no test covers watchPodCIDRs=false + a PodCIDR change (the crash-suppression gate); mutation-style coverage argument, low value.
True: no -race test exercises concurrent n.node access; but all accessors take n.mu, so it's a coverage gap not a bug — low value.
True: NodeConfig's handleChangeNode/handleDeleteNode/Run have no direct unit test despite the PR merging Add/Update and dropping AddFunc — coverage gap, low value.
True: the WaitForNamedCacheSync-failure ('can not sync node informer') branch has no test — minor coverage gap.
True: OnTopologyChange has no per-proxier test and metaProxier's v4/v6 fan-out is untested — but the logic is a trivial delegation; low value.

True: healthcheck_test builds NodeManager via NewNodeManager (real os.Exit) and discards err, so a future makeNode address change could kill the binary and a nil manager would nil-deref — real test fragility, but no current failure (makeNode is deterministic).
True: TestNewNodeManager coordinates its injector goroutine with time.Sleep handoffs racing the 10ms poll/1s timeout — a wall-clock anti-pattern / latent CI flake, low severity.
True: node/healthcheck test helpers discard Create/Update/constructor errors via _,_ so a setup failure surfaces confusingly downstream — diagnostic-quality-only nit.
